Alan: Pedestrian: I hope it won’t be bothering.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: In the second paragraph, the word garnered is closest in meaning to.
options: regulated ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 37-40 題 The Ebola outbreak in West Africa is the world’s deadliest to date and the World Health Organization has declared an international health emergency. Ebola virus disease (EVD) is a disease of humans and other primates caused by an Ebola virus. Symptoms such as a fever, sore throat, muscle pain and headaches start two days to three weeks after one contracts the virus. Typically, vomiting and diarrhea follow, along with decreased functioning of the liver and kidneys. Around this time, the infected may begin to bleed both within the body and externally. The virus may be acquired upon contact with blood or bodily fluids of an infected animal. In Africa, fruit bats are considered possible natural hosts of Ebola virus. They are believed to carry and spread the virus even without being infected. The monkeys were also observed to be carrying the virus. Spreading through the air has not been documented in the natural environment. Once human infection occurs, the disease may spread between people as well. Prevention includes decreasing the spread of the disease from infected animals to humans. This may be done by checking the infected animals, killing them and properly disposing of the bodies if the disease is discovered. Cooking meat properly and wearing protective clothing when handling meat may also be helpful, as are wearing protective clothing and washing hands when around a person with the disease. Banning or restricting the movement of animals from infected farms to other areas might reduce the spread of the disease. No specific treatment for the disease is yet available, but new drug therapies are being evaluated. The disease has a high mortality rate, often killing between 50% and 90% of those infected with the virus.EVD was first identified in Sudan and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. From 1976 (when it was first identified) through 2013, fewer than 1,000 people per year were infected. The largest outbreak to date is the ongoing 2014 West Africa Ebola outbreak, which is affecting Guinea, Sierra Leone, Liberia and Nigeria. As of August 2014, more than 1,750 suspected cases have been reported. Efforts are going on to develop a vaccine. Before any effective medical treatment can be done to those infected, people in Africa can’t do anything but wait.

options: Ebola-infected people would die soon within twenty-four hours.

It is only by contacting the infected animals that infection of people occurs. 第貳部分:非選擇題 ( 第 1 至 5 題,每題 4 分,共 20 分 ) 一、填充 說明:請依據中文之提示,在空格內填入適當之詞彙以完成句子。每格限填一字,並將空格內之 完整詞彙依序寫在「答案卷」之指定範圍內。
